facts:
- **Total AZ** misses budget by **-$693M (-1.4%)**, though still up +6.9% vs prior year
- **CVRM** is the only TA beating budget: **+$294M (+2.7%)**, driven by Farxiga (+$249M) and Brilinta (+$43M)
- **Oncology** is the biggest drag: **-$706M (-2.8%)** — Imfinzi (-$311M), Tagrisso (-$171M), Enhertu (-$187M), and Calquence (-$106M) all miss; only Lynparza beats (+$143M)
- **Rare Disease** misses by **-$152M (-2.3%)**, primarily Ultomiris (-$151M)
- **V&I** misses by **-$77M (-7.9%)** — Beyfortus underperforms across both US and CN
- **R&I** is nearly flat: **-$52M (-0.7%)** — Tezspire (-$78M) and Saphnelo (-$38M) offset by Symbicort (+$77M) and Fasenra (+$38M)
interpretation:
- The budget miss is almost entirely concentrated in **Oncology**, which accounts for ~102% of the total shortfall — CVRM's outperformance is the only thing keeping the company-level miss from being worse
- Oncology's miss is broad-based (5 of 7 brands below budget), suggesting either pricing pressure, slower-than-expected uptake, or market share headwinds rather than a single brand-specific issue
- Rare Disease's -$152M miss is notable given it's also down -16.5% vs prior year — Ultomiris is not growing fast enough to offset Soliris erosion at the portfolio level
recommendations:
- **Drill into Oncology brand-level drivers** — particularly Imfinzi (-$311M, -4.8%) and Enhertu (-$187M, -6.3%) to determine if the miss is US market share loss or CN pricing/access issues
- **Investigate Tezspire R&I miss** (-$78M, -7.3%) — the largest single-brand miss in R&I, in a fast-growing market (+40.5% category growth), which suggests an execution or access gap
- **Review Rare Disease trajectory**: Ultomiris is growing +12.5% YoY but still -$151M vs budget — validate whether the budget was set too aggressively or if patient conversion is lagging

visuals.title: FY 2025 Revenue vs Budget by Therapeutic Area

thinking_steps.content: I need to query total revenue by TA to understand variance vs budget, then decompose the total variance into TA-level contributions.

Total AZ is -$693.2M vs budget (-1.4%). Breaking down by TA: Oncology -$706.4M, CVRM +$293.6M, R&I -$51.8M, Rare Disease -$151.7M, V&I -$76.9M. CVRM is the only TA beating budget, while Oncology is the biggest drag.
